title: coussarea resinosa
intro: Coussarea resinosa, also known as the "Cinnamon Bark Tree," is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which is rich in resins and contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Coussarea resinosa include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Traditionally, the bark is prepared as a tea or infused in water to make a medicinal drink, and it can also be used to make a resin-based salve or ointment.
